title: fix_time_unix uses uint32_t causing Y2K38 overflow
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/app_interface/include/lichen/app_interface/app_interface.h:134

  bool fix_time_unix_valid;
  uint32_t fix_time_unix;

And in app_time_snapshot at line 159:
  bool unix_time_valid;
  uint32_t unix_time;

Unix timestamps stored as uint32_t will overflow on January 19, 2038 (Y2K38 problem). After this date:
- Valid timestamps will wrap to low values
- Time comparisons will be incorrect
- Timestamp arithmetic will produce unexpected results

This affects:
- lichen_app_location_time_snapshot.fix_time_unix (line 134)
- lichen_app_time_snapshot.unix_time (line 159)

For embedded systems with long deployment lifetimes, this is a design consideration. Either:
1. Document the 2038 limitation explicitly
2. Change to uint64_t or int64_t for future-proofing
close_reason: Changed uint32_t unix/fix_time_unix fields to int64_t/uint64_t in app_interface.h, hal.h samples+snaphots, coap_status, network_location structs, updated puck_location GNSS conversion, resolved related merge conflicts in coap_status.c; tests and CBOR encoding compatible. Y2K38 fixed.
